mission.title: n8n: Form submission returns 401 Unauthorized after upgrading to v2.2.4 (no Basic Auth enabled)
mission.description: Form submission returns 401 Unauthorized after upgrading to v2.2.4 (no Basic Auth enabled). Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current n8n deployment
steps.description:
Verify your n8n version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
helm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working n8n installation.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
## Describe the problem/error/question

After upgrading to v2.2.4, form submissions fail with 401 Unauthorized error. The form was working before the upgrade.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Form submission returns 401 Unauthorized after upgrading to…
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
[ ] I have updated to the latest stable version of n8n [please specify version number] and I am still experiencing this issue
[ ] Please close this issue – I am no longer experiencing it
[ ] Please close this issue – [please specify a reason]
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Form submission returns 401 Unauthorized after upgrading to…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/issues/24218

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
